questions.q: As a constitutional principle in India, secularism means the state does what with respect to religions?
options: Treats all religions equally and has no official religion
questions.explanation: Indian secularism means the state has no official religion and treats all religions equally.

questions.q: The word 'secular' was added to the Preamble of the Constitution by which amendment?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment of 1976 inserted the word 'secular' into the Preamble.

questions.q: In which year was the word 'secular' added to the Preamble?
questions.explanation: The word 'secular' was added to the Preamble in 1976 by the 42nd Amendment.

questions.q: Does India have an official or state religion under its Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Indian state has no official religion; it maintains equal distance from all faiths.

questions.q: Which fundamental right guarantees freedom of conscience and the freedom to profess, practise and propagate religion?
questions.explanation: Article 25 guarantees freedom of conscience and the right to profess, practise and propagate religion.

questions.q: Freedom of religion in the Constitution is part of which broad category of rights?
questions.explanation: Freedom of religion is one of the Fundamental Rights guaranteed in Part III of the Constitution.

questions.q: Under Indian secularism, can citizens of any religion hold public office?
options: Only those of one faith may
Only those who declare no religion may
questions.explanation: Equality before the law and secularism mean public office is open to citizens regardless of religion.

questions.q: Articles 25 to 28 of the Constitution collectively deal with which freedom?
questions.explanation: Articles 25 to 28 are the cluster of provisions guaranteeing the right to freedom of religion.

questions.q: Secularism in India is best described as the state's relationship being one of what towards all religions?
questions.explanation: Indian secularism is understood as equal respect for all religions and state neutrality among them.

questions.q: Secularism is regarded by the courts as part of what feature of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Supreme Court has held secularism to be part of the basic structure of the Constitution.

questions.q: Which Article prohibits the State from imposing any tax whose proceeds are used to promote or maintain any particular religion?
questions.explanation: Article 27 bars compelling any person to pay taxes for the promotion of any particular religion.

questions.q: Which Article relates to religious instruction in educational institutions wholly maintained out of State funds?
questions.explanation: Article 28 deals with the prohibition of religious instruction in institutions wholly maintained by State funds.

questions.q: Which Article grants every religious denomination the right to manage its own affairs in matters of religion?
questions.explanation: Article 26 grants religious denominations the freedom to manage their own religious affairs.

questions.q: Which Directive Principle calls upon the State to endeavour to secure a uniform civil code for citizens?
questions.explanation: Article 44, a Directive Principle, asks the State to endeavour to secure a uniform civil code.

questions.q: In the landmark 1994 S.R. Bommai case, the Supreme Court affirmed secularism as part of what?
options: The basic structure of the Constitution
questions.explanation: In the S.R. Bommai case (1994), the Supreme Court held that secularism forms part of the basic structure.

questions.q: Article 15 of the Constitution prohibits discrimination by the State on which grounds, among others?
options: Religion, race, caste, sex or place of birth
questions.explanation: Article 15 prohibits discrimination on grounds including religion, race, caste, sex and place of birth.

questions.q: Article 16 guarantees equality of opportunity in public employment and bars discrimination on grounds including which?
questions.explanation: Article 16 guarantees equality of opportunity in public employment irrespective of religion, among other grounds.

questions.q: The freedom of religion under Article 25 is subject to what general limits?
options: The discretion of the Prime Minister
questions.explanation: Article 25 makes the freedom of religion subject to public order, morality, health and other Part III provisions.

questions.q: Which constitutional provision allows the State to make laws regulating or restricting secular activity associated with religious practice?
questions.explanation: Article 25(2) permits the State to regulate secular activities associated with religious practice and provide for social welfare and reform.

questions.q: The cultural and educational rights protecting minorities' interests are found in which Articles?
questions.explanation: Articles 29 and 30 protect the cultural and educational rights of minorities.

questions.q: Article 30 guarantees minorities the right to do what?
options: Establish and administer educational institutions of their choice
questions.explanation: Article 30 grants religious and linguistic minorities the right to establish and administer educational institutions of their choice.

questions.q: Which words, besides 'secular', were added to the Preamble by the 42nd Amendment in 1976?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment added the words 'socialist', 'secular' and 'integrity' to the Preamble.

questions.q: In which 1994 case did a nine-judge bench of the Supreme Court extensively discuss secularism as a basic feature?
options: Maneka Gandhi v. Union of India
Golak Nath v. State of Punjab
S.R. Bommai v. Union of India
A.K. Gopalan v. State of Madras
questions.explanation: The nine-judge bench in S.R. Bommai v. Union of India (1994) discussed secularism as a basic feature of the Constitution.

questions.q: Even before 'secular' was added to the Preamble, which fundamental rights already reflected the secular character of the State?
options: The right to property under Article 31
The right against exploitation under Article 23
The right to constitutional remedies under Article 32
The freedom of religion under Articles 25 to 28
questions.explanation: Articles 25 to 28 embodied India's secular character from the Constitution's commencement, before the word was inserted in 1976.

questions.q: Article 290A deals with the secular State making annual payments to certain funds connected with which institutions?
options: Certain Devaswom (temple) funds in Kerala and Tamil Nadu
questions.explanation: Article 290A provides for annual payments by Kerala and Tamil Nadu to specified Devaswom (temple) funds, a financial provision in Part XII of the Constitution.

questions.q: The phrase that the State shall observe a position of neutrality among religions is often linked to which Western model that India did not strictly adopt?
options: A strict wall of separation between church and state
questions.explanation: Indian secularism differs from the strict separation model, allowing the State principled engagement with religions while staying neutral.

questions.q: Which Article empowers the State to throw open Hindu religious institutions of a public character to all classes and sections of Hindus, reflecting social reform within secular governance?
questions.explanation: Article 25(2)(b) allows the State to provide for opening Hindu religious institutions of a public character to all sections of Hindus.

questions.q: Abolition of untouchability under Article 17, which supports equal dignity across communities, is enforced as what?
questions.explanation: Article 17 abolishes untouchability and is a justiciable fundamental right reinforcing equality across communities.

questions.q: Which constitutional remedy allows citizens to approach the Supreme Court directly to enforce the freedom of religion?
questions.explanation: Article 32 enables citizens to move the Supreme Court directly to enforce fundamental rights, including freedom of religion.

questions.q: Religious instruction wholly prohibited under Article 28 applies to institutions of which kind?
options: Those wholly maintained out of State funds
questions.explanation: Article 28 bars religious instruction only in educational institutions wholly maintained out of State funds, with stated exceptions.
